scenario_spec.signal_type: Single-user SISO complex-baseband binary occupancy sensing with non-coherent energy detection.

test_scenarios.name: ideal_awgn_np_validation
test_scenarios.description: Use known \(\sigma_w^2\) to set analytic NP threshold at \(P_{fa}=0.01\) and compare theoretical and empirical \(P_d\).

test_scenarios.name: fixed_pfa_roc_points
test_scenarios.description: Evaluate \(P_d\) at fixed \(P_{fa}\in\{0.01,0.05,0.1\}\) across SNR to verify operating-point consistency.

test_scenarios.name: full_roc_curve
test_scenarios.description: Sweep threshold to generate ROC curves and AUC at selected SNR values from the task grid.

test_scenarios.name: noise_uncertainty_sensitivity
test_scenarios.description: Apply threshold computed with assumed noise power while true noise differs by \(\pm1\) dB to expose ED sensitivity.

scenario_spec.generation_notes: All Monte Carlo samples are i.i.d. complex Gaussian with vectorized generation and fixed seed for reproducibility.

system_model_doc: For each sensing interval, the receiver observes \(\mathbf{y}\in\mathbb{C}^{N\times1}\) with \(N=256\) under \(\mathcal{H}_0:\mathbf{y}=\mathbf{w}\) and \(\mathcal{H}_1:\mathbf{y}=\mathbf{s}+\mathbf{w}\), where \(\mathbf{w}\sim\mathcal{CN}(\mathbf{0},\sigma_w^2\mathbf{I}_N)\) and \(\mathbf{s}\sim\mathcal{CN}(\mathbf{0},\gamma\sigma_w^2\mathbf{I}_N)\); the sufficient statistic is normalized energy \(U=\sigma_w^{-2}\sum_{n=1}^{N}|y[n]|^2\), with NP rule \(U\gtrless_{\mathcal{H}_0}^{\mathcal{H}_1}\eta\) where \(\eta=\tfrac12F^{-1}_{\chi^2_{2N}}(1-P_{fa})\), and theoretical detection is \(P_d(\gamma)=Q_N(\sqrt{2N\gamma},\sqrt{2\eta})=\Pr\{\chi'^2_{2N}(2N\gamma)>2\eta\}\).

math_formulation.problem_type: Constrained binary hypothesis test (Neyman-Pearson) with known-noise energy statistic.

objective.description: Maximize detection probability subject to a false-alarm upper bound using a scalar threshold on the sufficient statistic.

variables.name: received sample
variables.description: Observed complex baseband sample at index \(n\in\{1,\dots,N\}\).

variables.name: normalized energy statistic
variables.description: Sufficient statistic for NP testing under i.i.d. circular Gaussian noise.

variables.name: decision threshold
variables.description: Scalar threshold set by the \(P_{fa}\) constraint.

variables.name: SNR
variables.description: Linear SNR under \(\mathcal{H}_1\), related to dB by \(\gamma=10^{\mathrm{SNR}_{dB}/10}\).

variables.name: noise variance
variables.description: Known or calibrated AWGN variance used for normalization and thresholding.

variables.name: samples per interval
variables.description: Fixed sensing-window length with task value \(N=256\).

constraints.description: False-alarm probability must satisfy the NP design constraint.
Detector must use the specified energy statistic.
Binary decision rule maps statistic to occupancy decision.

key_formulas.name: H0_statistic_distribution
key_formulas.description: Central distribution used for threshold inversion.

key_formulas.name: H1_statistic_distribution
key_formulas.description: Distribution under random Gaussian signal model for analytic \(P_d\).

key_formulas.name: np_threshold
key_formulas.description: Closed-form NP threshold from central chi-square quantile.

key_formulas.name: theoretical_detection_probability
key_formulas.description: Equivalent chi-square CDF and Marcum-Q forms for \(P_d\).

key_formulas.name: empirical_estimators
key_formulas.description: Monte Carlo estimates with \(T=10^5\) trials per SNR.

assumptions: Samples are i.i.d. within each sensing interval and the channel is AWGN, frequency-flat, and memoryless.
Noise variance is known for the main NP design and only perturbed in robustness checks.
Primary signal is modeled as circular complex Gaussian with power \(\gamma\sigma_w^2\) under \(\mathcal{H}_1\).
Single-interval fixed-sample detection is the core scope, not sequential quickest detection.

model_properties.convexity: Monotone one-dimensional threshold design with exact quantile inversion rather than a nonconvex search.
model_properties.special_structure: Sufficient-statistic reduction to scalar energy with chi-square/gamma distributional structure under both hypotheses.

math_formulation.formulation_markdown: The NP detector reduces the \(N\)-sample complex observation to scalar energy \(U\), so design is a one-parameter test where \(\eta\) is chosen by exact inversion of the \(\mathcal{H}_0\) chi-square CDF to enforce \(P_{fa}\le0.01\), and performance prediction uses the closed-form \(\mathcal{H}_1\) survival function (equivalently Marcum-\(Q\)) across the prescribed SNR grid for direct Monte Carlo cross-validation.

design_implications: Exploit sufficient-statistic structure by reducing each interval to scalar energy, enabling exact NP thresholding without iterative optimization.
Use chi-square/gamma closed forms to generate theoretical curves before simulation, then treat Monte Carlo as validation rather than parameter fitting.
Maintain O(N) per-interval implementation to satisfy low-compute real-time constraints.
evaluation_focus: Verify \(P_{fa}\le 0.01\) and report \(P_d\) versus SNR from -20 dB to 0 dB with mandatory emphasis at -10 dB.
Track absolute theory-simulation \(P_d\) gap against target \(\le 0.02\) where specified.
Overlay the Neyman-Pearson optimality benchmark as a reference bound in performance plots.
Report ROC/AUC and runtime per interval, plus secondary noise-uncertainty sensitivity.
implementation_risks: Finite-trial tail estimation may bias \(\widehat{P}_{fa}\) near 0.01 if vectorization or RNG handling is incorrect.
Noise-variance mismatch directly shifts effective threshold and can significantly degrade ED detection at low SNR.
Inconsistent normalization between theory and simulation (factor-of-2 chi-square scaling) can create false discrepancy.

proposed_algorithm.name: Closed-Form NP Energy Detector with Vectorized Monte Carlo Validation
proposed_algorithm.summary: Compute analytic NP thresholds and theoretical \(P_d\), then validate with vectorized Monte Carlo and ROC generation under ideal and noise-mismatch settings.

algorithm_steps.name: Initialize constants and grids
algorithm_steps.description: Set fixed task parameters and convert SNR dB grid to linear values for all subsequent computations.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Use np.array for dB grid and gamma_grid = 10**(snr_db/10).

algorithm_steps.name: Set random seed and noise variance reference
algorithm_steps.description: Fix reproducibility and define assumed noise variance used in normalization and thresholding.

algorithm_steps.name: Compute NP thresholds for required Pfa points
algorithm_steps.description: Obtain analytic thresholds from central chi-square quantiles for the fixed and ROC operating points.

algorithm_steps.name: Compute theoretical Pd curves
algorithm_steps.description: Evaluate closed-form \(P_d\) over SNR for each fixed \(P_{fa}\) threshold.

algorithm_steps.name: Generate H0 Monte Carlo samples
algorithm_steps.description: Create \(T\times N\) i.i.d. complex Gaussian noise matrices for each mismatch scenario.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Use rng.standard_normal((T,N)) for real and imag parts, then combine.

algorithm_steps.name: Generate H1 Monte Carlo samples per SNR
algorithm_steps.description: For each SNR and mismatch case, generate Gaussian signal plus noise observations.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Vectorize over trials and samples; loop only over SNR points.

algorithm_steps.name: Compute normalized energy statistics
algorithm_steps.description: Reduce raw complex samples to scalar sufficient statistics for H0 and H1.

algorithm_steps.name: Apply NP decision rule
algorithm_steps.description: Threshold statistics to produce binary decisions under each hypothesis.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Use boolean comparisons and astype(np.float64) for averaging.

algorithm_steps.name: Estimate empirical Pfa and Pd
algorithm_steps.description: Compute Monte Carlo probabilities and theory-simulation gaps for reporting targets.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Use np.mean over trial axis and np.abs difference.

algorithm_steps.name: Generate full ROC and AUC
algorithm_steps.description: Sweep threshold grid to compute ROC points and numerical AUC at selected SNR values.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Use np.quantile for threshold grid, vectorized comparisons, and np.trapz for AUC.

algorithm_steps.name: Measure runtime and operation count
algorithm_steps.description: Compute per-interval runtime and analytical operation complexity for the primary detector.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Use time.perf_counter and simple scalar arithmetic.

baseline_algorithms.name: NP energy detector with analytic threshold
baseline_algorithms.summary: Classical reference detector using exact chi-square threshold inversion at target \(P_{fa}\).

baseline_algorithms.name: Monte Carlo-calibrated CFAR energy threshold
baseline_algorithms.summary: Empirical threshold baseline that estimates \(\eta\) from noise-only samples at desired \(P_{fa}\).

baseline_algorithms.name: Neyman-Pearson optimality for fixed P_fa when using energy as the chosen test statistic under AWGN with known noise variance.
baseline_algorithms.summary: Use NP lemma result as analytical benchmark curve and interpret any implementable detector against this optimum within the chosen statistic family.
baseline_algorithms.numpy_hint: analytical formula — compute and plot as reference

baseline_algorithms.name: Cyclostationary feature detector (optional reference)
baseline_algorithms.summary: Complexity-robustness comparison baseline using cyclic autocorrelation magnitude.
baseline_algorithms.numpy_hint: Use np.exp, np.conj, and optionally np.fft.fft for acceleration.

complexity.per_iteration: Primary detector evaluation is O(N) per interval; full study cost is O(|SNR|·T·N) for Monte Carlo sweeps.
complexity.dominant_operation: Accumulation of squared magnitudes for energy statistics over T×N samples (and threshold sweeps for ROC).
